sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangoes grown from Mangifera indica L., of the Anacardiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangoes for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangoes grown from Mangifera indica L., of the Anacardiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangoes for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangoes grown from Mangifera indica L., of the Anacardiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangoes for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
sections.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the mangoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean, practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of damage caused by pests; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; free of damage caused by low temperatures; free of black necrotic stains or trails; free of marked bruising; sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness. When a peduncle is present, it shall be no longer than 1.0 cm.

When a peduncle is present, it shall be no longer than 1.0 cm.

page_spans.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the mangoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean, practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of damage caused by pests; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; free of damage caused by low temperatures; free of black necrotic stains or trails; free of marked bruising; sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness. When a peduncle is present, it shall be no longer than 1.0 cm.

sections.title: The development and condition of the mangoes must be such as to enable them
path_titles: The development and condition of the mangoes must be such as to enable them
sections.text: to ensure a continuation of the maturation process until they reach the appropriate degree of maturity corresponding to the varietal characteristics; to withstand transport and handling; and to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination. In relation to the evolution of maturing, the colour may vary according to variety.

sections.title: "Extra" Class
sections.text: Mangoes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the weight of the fruit, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Weight (in grams)
A 200 - 350
B 351 - 550
C 551 - 800
The maximum permissible difference between fruit in the same package belonging to one of the above mentioned size groups shall be 75, 100 and 125 g respectively. The minimum weight of mangoes must not be less than 200 g.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
2 > 2.2 > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=2
Mangoes in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight defects in shape;
- slight skin defects due to rubbing or sunburn, suberized stains due to resin exudation (elongated trails included) and healed bruises not exceeding 3, 4, 5 cm² for size groups A, B, C respectively.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text:
This class includes mangoes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the mangoes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:
- defects in shape;
- skin defects due to rubbing or sunburn, suberized stains due to resin exudation (elongated trails included) and healed bruises not exceeding 5, 6, 7 cm² for size groups A, B, C respectively.
In Classes I and II, scattered suberized rusty lenticels, as well as yellowing of green varieties due to exposure to direct sunlight, not exceeding 40 % of the surface and not showing any signs of necrosis are allowed.
sections.retrieval_text:

In Classes I and II, scattered suberized rusty lenticels, as well as yellowing of green varieties due to exposure to direct sunlight, not exceeding 40 % of the surface and not showing any signs of necrosis are allowed.

sections.title: SIZE TOLERANCES
sections.text: For all classes, 10 % by number or weight of mangoes in each package are permitted to be outside (above or below) the group size range by 50 % of the maximum permissible difference for the group. In the smallest size range, mangoes must not be less than 180 g and for those in the largest size range a maximum of 925 g applies, as follows:

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Mangoes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Mangoes shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the mangoes. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193-1995).

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions o f this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1 -1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangoes grown from Mangifera indica L., of the Anacardiaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

prefilter.reason: It specifies a regulatory exclusion relevant for standard scope.

classification.sentence: Mangoes for industrial processing are excluded.
classification.function: Exclusion from scope
classification.pattern: Subject + are excluded + specified use
classification.justification: Expresses an exclusion from scope, structurally a negative prohibition; no strong template match.
notes: Short exclusion sentence with clear prohibition modality.

sentences.original: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the mangoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean, practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of damage caused by pests; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; free of damage caused by low temperatures; free of black necrotic stains or trails; free of marked bruising; sufficiently developed and display satisfactory ripeness.

classification.justification: Standard conditional prohibition regarding size limit; fits broadly conditional obligation pattern with numerical limit.
notes: Condition triggers obligation of size restriction.

sentences.original: to ensure a continuation of the maturation process until they reach the appropriate degree of maturity corresponding to the varietal characteristics; to withstand transport and handling; and to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sentences.original: Mangoes in this class must be of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.function: Requirement with Conditional Exception
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

classification.pattern: Subject package or lot identified, followed by must + be + free-of state describing quality criteria

components.subject: Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk)
components.obligation: must be free of all foreign matter and smell

classification.justification: Obligation with clear normative wording and quality assurance function; structure similar to common 'must be free of' templates but with variation in subject expression.
notes: Adapted template due to inclusion of alternative subject phrasing for bulk produce.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause
classification.function: Conditional obligation combined with conditional permission
classification.pattern: If [Condition], [Obligation] and [Permission] on the subject applies

components.Condition: If the produce is not visible from the outside
components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

sentences.original: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment.

classification.sentence: For produce transported in bulk these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.
classification.function: Mandate accompanying documentation particulars for bulk produce transport
classification.pattern: Conditional phrase describing transport mode, followed by obligation to include particulars on accompanying document

components.obligation: particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods

classification.justification: Sentence expresses obligation conditioned on bulk transport and documentation requirements, with conditional phrase structure uncommon in known templates.
notes: Condition expressed by transport mode triggers obligation on documentation.

prefilter.reason: Contains normative content related to origin and place names relevant to classification.

classification.sentence: Country of origin and, optionally, district where grown or national, regional or local place name.
classification.function: Optional specification of place-related attributes
classification.pattern: [Main subject] and, optionally, [additional attributes or qualifiers] relating to place names.

components.optional_component: district where grown or national, regional or local place name

classification.justification: The sentence provides optional information attributes related to place names without imposing obligations, conditions, prohibitions, or limits, thus fitting a Permission modality with an informational communicative function. It does not match any known templates; its structure is a main subject with an optional extension listing place-related descriptors.
notes: The sentence is fragmentary and lacks a verb, which limits clarity on its exact communicative function. It appears to allow optional inclusion of certain details but does not explicitly state permission in a normative form.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MANGOES (CODEX STAN 184-1993)
pages.summary: Página com título do documento e cabeçalho. Identificados níveis hierárquicos 1 a 3 com numeração clara. Texto associado a cada nó conforme conteúdo presente. Continuidade marcada para seções não finalizadas na página, incluindo a Classe Extra na subseção 2.2.1.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 184
pages.summary: Página contém hierarquia detalhada com níveis 1 a 3 claramente identificados. Títulos com numeração e caixa alta foram mapeados com textos associados respectivos. Marcação adequada de continuidade, especialmente para seções que se prolongam na próxima página. Cabeçalho e título do documento identificados para contexto.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 184
pages.summary: Página apresenta níveis 1 a 3 claramente estruturados com títulos numerados e em caixa alta. Texto associado devidamente segmentado por seção. A seção 4.1.3 está em continuidade de conteúdo da página anterior. Seção 6 sinaliza continuidade para próxima página. Cabeçalho e título do documento identificados e excluídos do conteúdo das seções.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 184
pages.summary: Esta página contém 3 seções principais no nível 1: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS (6.2), CONTAMINANTS (7) e HYGIENE (8). A seção 6.2 possui cinco subseções numeradas no nível 2, todas claramente estruturadas e com texto associado. A seção 7 tem um conteúdo introdutório e uma subseção 7.2. A seção 8 apresenta texto principal e subseção 8.2. A seção 8 indica continuidade para a próxima página. O cabeçalho e título do documento foram identificados e excluídos do texto das seções para garantir qualidade no RAG.
